personal.name: Fernando Pinto Villarroel
personal.title: Software Engineer & Linguist
personal.subtitle: Navigating the tech universe through hands-on projects and creative solutions.
personal.bio: Software Engineer with 4 years of hands-on experience in full-stack development. I bring together technical precision with linguistic understanding to create innovative solutions. Currently pursuing my degree while building scalable applications and exploring the intersection of technology and human communication.

projects.title: Paperless-NGX Microservices Migration
projects.description: Migration of the open-source Paperless-NGX legacy monolithic system to a scalable microservices architecture. The legacy system (monolithic with OCR, Search modules, and AngularJS frontend) is gradually modernized using the Strangler Fig pattern with ACL (Anti-Corruption Layer) for seamless integration. Key microservices include OCR (with PostgreSQL) and Search (with Elasticsearch). Modern architecture supports millions of users, featuring a React SPA frontend with TypeScript, Vite, Zustand, and Tanstack Router; API Gateway using Ocelot in C# ASP.NET Core; OCR and Search microservices in C# and TypeScript respectively; RabbitMQ for event-driven communication; and bidirectional synchronization via ACL background service. This is the most complete full-stack project, incorporating observability with LGTM and Sentry, Docker deployment, and Supabase CDN.
features: Gradual microservices migration using Strangler Fig and ACL patterns
Bidirectional synchronization between legacy and modern systems
Scalable architecture with independent microservices
Event-driven communication with RabbitMQ
Modern React SPA with advanced state management
Observability and deployment tools

projects.title: Battle Tanks
projects.description: Real-time multiplayer game replicating the classic Battle Tanks experience. Built as a full-stack application with Angular frontend using SignalR store for state management, and C# ASP.NET Core backend implementing CQRS pattern with MediatR. Features hexagonal architecture in backend and feature-based architecture in frontend. Real-time communication via MQTT for secondary features (chat, powerups) and SignalR for core gameplay (movement, shooting, collisions). Implements professional database practices including sharding, partitioning, and replication with PostgreSQL, plus Redis caching for GET endpoints. Docker infrastructure. Testing suite with Apache JMeter, Postman, K6, and more. Focus on real-time performance and scalability for concurrent users. Includes additional Angular Map Creator tool for advanced map design with JSON import/export and tile editing.
features: Real-time multiplayer gameplay with synchronized state
Advanced architectural patterns: CQRS, Hexagonal, Feature-based
Real-time communication with MQTT and SignalR
Professional database optimization: sharding, partitioning, replication
Performance testing and monitoring tools
Additional Map Creator tool for game development

projects.title: PixPro
projects.description: An AI-driven image processing platform that empowers authenticated users to upload and batch-process photos through powerful AI models (ranging from Cloudinary's enhancement APIs and Gemini-powered filters to YOLO-based object detection) within a project-organized workspace. The system offers real-time WebSocket notifications, secure CDN-delivered downloads, and customizable parameter presets to streamline and safeguard every step of the workflow.
features: Project-based organization with unlimited custom-named projects.
Selectable AI processing categories (Image Improvements, Image Effects, Object Detection)
Intuitive image upload (JPG/JPEG/PNG up to 20 MB) with batch uploads (up to 5 images).
Real-time WebSocket notifications showing status updates.

WebSocket-based communication for instant player interactions.

projects.title: Warehouse Monitoring System
projects.description: The Warehouse Monitoring System is an industrial IoT solution designed to continuously monitor and control environmental conditions in storage facilities by tracking temperature and humidity levels via DHT22 sensors connected to an ESP32 microcontroller, transmitting data to cloud services for real-time visualization, automatically activating cooling mechanisms and sending multichannel alerts when predefined safety thresholds are exceeded, and providing manual override capabilities through a secure web dashboard.
features: Real-time temperature and humidity detection with live dashboard visualization on both desktop and mobile devices.
Automated cooling system activation whenever readings exceed 37°C or 70% humidity, ensuring rapid response to critical conditions.
Multi-channel alert system combining visual LED indicators, an audible buzzer, and email notifications to guarantee that alerts are always received.
Remote command and control through a web dashboard, allowing facility managers to manually open or close cooling systems as needed.
Cloud-based data analytics and customizable dashboards powered by TagoIO for advanced visualization, historical data trends, and rule-based alerts.

projects.title: Gaming Audio Processing System
projects.description: A concurrent and parallel audio processing platform built for Programming 7 coursework to master async, parallel, and concurrent programming across frontend and backend. The C# ASP.NET Core backend follows Hexagonal + Clean Architecture with CQRS (MediatR) and uses SignalR for real-time notifications, SemaphoreSlim and Parallel.ForEachAsync for concurrent processing pipelines (compression → transcription → summarization / DSP filtering), and spawns CPU-bound Python processes (Whisper, BART CNN) and FFmpeg + NAudio + MathNet for audio DSP filtering. The React + TypeScript + Vite frontend implements a Web Worker pool, assigning one worker per audio for fully parallel client-side processing with Transformers.js (Whisper Tiny for transcription, MobileBERT MNLI for gaming content domain validation). Processed files (original, compressed, filtered) are stored on a Dockerized MinIO instance. Performance benchmarked with K6 — parallel processing achieved 2× speedup over sequential, validated using Amdahl's Law.
features: Web Worker pool for parallel client-side audio processing — one worker assigned per audio file
Client-side AI pipeline: transcription (Whisper Tiny) → gaming domain validation (MobileBERT MNLI) via Transformers.js
Backend concurrent pipeline: compression (FFmpeg) → transcription (Whisper) → summarization (BART CNN) / DSP filtering (NAudio + MathNet)
CPU-bound Python process integration for AI inference (Whisper, BART CNN) and Amdahl's Law-based parallelization
Real-time SignalR notifications and batch audio upload support with concurrent queues and cancellation tokens
K6 performance benchmarks proving 2× speedup of parallel processing over sequential baseline

projects.title: Startup Idea Validator
projects.description: An MVP web platform for crowdsourced startup idea validation, combining community feedback with AI-powered market analysis. Users submit business ideas and receive community reactions (likes, comments, 'I'd pay for it' engagement), while an integrated AI engine uses SerpAPI to retrieve real-time market data and OpenAI GPT models to generate structured validation reports covering risks, opportunities, and competitive landscape. Contributed to: AI integration pipeline, overall UI/UX redesign, Supabase PostgreSQL database architecture and OAuth authentication (Google IDP), and a fully functional PayPal SDK payment integration enabling users to purchase in-app credits.
features: Community idea validation with likes, comments, and 'I'd pay for it' engagement signals
AI-powered market analysis using SerpAPI (real-time search context) + OpenAI GPT (risks, opportunities, competitive landscape)
Fully functional PayPal SDK integration for purchasing in-app credits
Supabase PostgreSQL database with Google OAuth authentication
UI/UX redesign and Next.js App Router architecture

projects.title: Wira AI
projects.description: AI-powered air pollution analysis platform that won 1st place in NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025, Cochabamba, Bolivia. Team CloudCatchers developed a React frontend with JavaScript, Vite, and TailwindCSS, integrating multiple APIs including OpenWeatherMap and NASA TEMPO data. Features data cleaning, ML model training for weather predictions, and integrated Gemini LLM for clear explanations of air pollution globally, by country, and city. Includes pages for news, actions, predictions (self-retraining with N8N in cloud), chat with LLM, analytics, etc. Proposal slides available at the demo link.
features: 1st place NASA Space Apps Challenge winner
ML model for air pollution predictions
LLM integration for pollution explanations
Multi-API data integration and cleaning
Self-retraining prediction system
Analytics and news platform

projects.title: GPA Calculator for Jala University
projects.description: A feature-rich cumulative and semester GPA calculator built specifically for Jala University students, where each cohort follows a distinct academic program structure — making standard GPA tools unreliable. Built with React, Next.js, TypeScript, Zustand, and TailwindCSS following a feature-based architecture. Key pages include: a dashboard displaying CGPA, statistics, and honor distinctions (cum laude); a grades page for registering scores per cohort, semester, and subject (with retake support); automatic grade import from the official SIS PDF export via client-side text extraction and parsing using a Web Worker; full data portability via JSON import/export (all data stored in localStorage only — never on a server or database); a statistics page with Recharts visualizations; and a forecasting page for modeling grade combinations needed to reach a target CGPA. Also features dark/light theme toggle, internationalization across 3 locales (/en, /es, /pt), and a React Joyride guided tour for new user onboarding. A personal open-source project built to help the Jala University student community.
features: Dashboard with CGPA, semester GPA, statistics, and honor distinction (cum laude) display
Grade registration per cohort, semester, and subject — including multiple retake attempts
Automatic grade import from official SIS PDF export via Web Worker text extraction and parsing
JSON import/export with full localStorage privacy (no server or database involved)
Grade forecasting page to model score combinations needed to reach a target CGPA
Internationalization (EN/ES/PT), dark/light theme, and React Joyride guided onboarding tour
Progressive Web App (PWA) support, allowing installation as a desktop or mobile app

projects.title: JalaLibrary
projects.description: Library management system with dual user roles (normal users and admins). Admins can perform CRUD operations on books, including managing available copies for loans. Normal users can view books, search, filter, sort, borrow books, add to wishlist, and receive notifications for changes in availability. Built as a single React frontend with Next.js using app router and file-based routing, Material UI, TailwindCSS, and Redux Toolkit for state management. Connects to Supabase PostgreSQL database with triggers, functions, indexes, and RLS policies for security. Includes Supabase Auth with email/password and Google OAuth, JWT and refresh token handling. Features light/dark theme. Full-stack architecture where Next.js handles SSR and abstracts Supabase calls.
features: Dual role system: admin and user functionalities
Advanced book management with CRUD operations
Wishlist and notification system
Supabase integration with security policies
Authentication with multiple providers
Responsive design with theme support

Frontend project with external APIs.

User authentication with JWT handling

Reviewers can add comments but cannot modify content. Statistics for content planned by week, month, etc.

projects.title: My Reading Town
projects.description: A 2D mobile city-building game developed with Flutter, Dart, and the Flame engine that gamifies real-world reading habits. Players earn resources (coins, gems, wood, metal) by logging book pages read, which are spent to construct buildings (houses, parks, hospitals, water and electricity plants, decorations) that fulfill the needs of their animal villagers (cats, rabbits, dogs). Each inhabitant displays emotional states with speech bubbles showing unmet needs or their favorite author when happy. Includes mini-games (guess the author, match the book character with their description) that reward special time-limited power-ups: an extra builder (1 hour), double construction speed (1 day), or an instant happiness boost for any villager (1 day). Features a branched mission system (construction, villager, and reading branches), land expansion with gems/coins, construction acceleration with gems, an advanced reading log with book search (internet or manual entry) and statistics, an experience system, and full localization in 5 languages (EN/ES/PT/FR/IT). Inspired by Clash of Clans, reimagined to encourage younger generations to build real reading habits.
features: Resource economy tied to real reading: earn coins, gems, wood, and metal by logging book pages
Villager happiness system with emotional states, speech bubbles, needs display, and favorite author quotes
Mini-games (author guessing, book character matching) with time-limited power-up rewards
Branched mission system across construction, villager, and reading progression tracks
Advanced reading log with internet book search, manual entry, reading statistics, and history
Localization in 5 languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, and Italian
